query: As of early July 2026, what is the current national norovirus category on the WastewaterSCAN dashboard right now? The NBC News article says national HIGH. Is norovirus currently rising or falling in the US wastewater in late June/early July 2026? What is the most recent trend and is the off-season surge continuing or declining?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of my findings:

**Current WastewaterSCAN category (early July 2026): HIGH nationally** — confirming the NBC News article's "national HIGH" claim.

**Is it rising or falling?** The signals are genuinely mixed depending on source vintage:
- NBC News and iHeart (late May) reported it **rising** (especially Northeast/Midwest)
- The more recent **Newsweek** snapshot describes it as **"high but stable" — no clear up/down trend in the past 21 days** (a plateau)
- Virus Watcher (reviewed July 7) still calls it "sharply rising" but projects a **July peak with decline into August**

**Best characterization:** The off-season surge is at a **HIGH plateau near/at peak** — continuing at elevated levels but not clearly accelerating nationally, with commentary pointing to a likely July peak and expected decline into August.

**Important context:** Despite the "HIGH" wastewater label, CDC NoroSTAT (through June 11) shows only 1,287 outbreaks this season — about **half** last season's 2,596 — and within the middle 50% of the 2012–2025 historical baseline, so it's not unusually high by historical standards. This is an unusual summer surge partly driven by the GII.17 variant.

**Caveat:** The live WastewaterSCAN dashboard renders via JavaScript and couldn't be scraped directly, so the category/trend are drawn from news outlets citing that dashboard, with differing snapshot dates explaining much of the rising-vs-stable discrepancy.

sources.title: Vomiting virus is spreading across most of the U.S.
sources.snippet: Recent wastewater surveillance reveals an upward trend to 'high' levels of norovirus across much of the country, with rates currently rising in the Northeast.

sources.title: Norovirus Map Shows Where 'Vomiting Virus' Levels Are High Across US
sources.snippet: National level: High but stable; no clear upward or downward trend in the past 21 days - sustained transmission. High activity in Midwest and Northeast; medium in West and South.

sources.title: High Levels Of 'Winter Vomiting Disease' Found In Wastewater Across The US
sources.snippet: National category 'High'; rising sharply, levels up ~45% since November with sharpest increases in Midwest and Northeast; driven by GII.17 variant.

sources.title: NoroSTAT Data | Norovirus | CDC
sources.snippet: During August 1, 2025-June 11, 2026, there were 1,287 norovirus outbreaks vs 2,596 same period last season; within the middle 50% of 2012-2025 outbreaks.

sources.title: Norovirus Outbreak Tracker (2026) | Virus Watcher
sources.snippet: Last reviewed 2026-07-07: norovirus cases sharply rising across US per CDC data and wastewater; officials urge hygiene.

sources.title: Norovirus Summer 2026: Which States Are Seeing the Highest Activity?
sources.snippet: Summer norovirus wave active; elevated activity in at least 12 states (Utah, Texas, Ohio, North Carolina leading); peak likely July, declining into August.

sources.title: WastewaterSCAN Dashboard
sources.snippet: National norovirus (Noro_G2) dashboard; loads dynamically via JavaScript.
